insights.description: Time-of-day edge: 'morning' (6:00-12:00 UTC) wins 71% vs 'night' at 15% (spread: 56%)
insights.actionable_suggestion: Boost confidence for trades during morning (6:00-12:00 UTC). Consider raising confidence floor during night by 5-10%.

insights.description: Significant time-of-day weakness: 'night' (0:00-6:00 UTC) has only 15% WR over 13 trades
insights.actionable_suggestion: Consider pausing trading or raising confidence floor to 75%+ during night (0:00-6:00 UTC).

insights.description: Size edge: larger positions (>5.0x) have 57% WR vs 31% for smaller. Higher conviction trades are more profitable.
insights.actionable_suggestion: High-conviction sizing is working. Consider increasing sizes on high-confidence setups and further reducing sizes on low-confidence ones.

insights.description: Time-of-day edge: 'evening' (18:00-24:00 UTC) wins 65% vs 'night' at 10% (spread: 55%)
insights.actionable_suggestion: Boost confidence for trades during evening (18:00-24:00 UTC). Consider raising confidence floor during night by 5-10%.
insights.invalidation_reason: Post-dedup evidence invalidated. INSIGHTS_DEDUP_PRUNING (2026-05-18) removed 89 duplicate entries that inflated this WR. Meta_learning analysis confirmed evening=33% WR (not 65%) and afternoon=27% WR (not 64%), n=27. idea_2238f358 and idea_920b3e13 explicitly invalidated based on this. Marking insight to prevent re-proposal of inverted rules.

insights.description: Side bias: 74% of trades are LONG but LONG WR is 30% vs 77% for the other side
insights.actionable_suggestion: Reduce LONG bias — we're taking too many LONG trades that underperform. Consider adding a side-balance check to the ensemble.
insights.invalidation_reason: STALE_DATA_CONTRADICTED: 10d dataset (n=965 trades) shows only 12% LONG trades, not 74%. The LONG-heavy period this was built on no longer reflects current portfolio mix. Critically, this insight was suppressing SOL_LONG 70-80% confidence signals (62% WR, avg +$20.88, n=208) — the single best sub-condition in the dataset. Run 90 auto-fix (conf=82% >= 75% threshold). Expected impact: +$418-$1,059 EV/100d from restored SOL_LONG signals.

insights.description: Significant time-of-day weakness: 'morning' (6:00-12:00 UTC) has only 14% WR over 7 trades
insights.actionable_suggestion: Consider pausing trading or raising confidence floor to 75%+ during morning (6:00-12:00 UTC).
insights.invalidation_reason: DIRECT_CONTRADICTION: Insight 1 (same file, ts=1776987543.82, n=20, conf=0.73) shows morning 06:00-12:00 UTC at 71% WR. This insight claims 14% WR on only n=7 trades. Smaller sample (n=7) contradicts larger (n=20). Morning 68-71% WR confirmed in audit backtest data. Run 90 auto-fix (conf=82% >= 75% threshold). Invalidating to prevent agents from suppressing morning signals.

insights.description: Size bias detected: larger positions (>5.0x) have 36% WR vs 46% for smaller positions. Avg PnL: $-1.38 vs $+1.36
insights.actionable_suggestion: Reduce position sizes — larger positions are losing more often. Consider capping leverage or adding a size penalty to the confidence calculation.

insights.description: Strategy concentration: 'ensemble' accounts for 94% of all trades (47/50). WR: 45%
insights.actionable_suggestion: Diversify: over-reliance on 'ensemble' creates fragility. Consider reducing its ensemble weight or boosting underrepresented strategies.

insights.description: Size edge: larger positions (>6.0x) have 58% WR vs 35% for smaller. Higher conviction trades are more profitable.
insights.actionable_suggestion: High-conviction sizing is working. Consider increasing sizes on high-confidence setups and further reducing sizes on low-confidence ones.
Reduce weight for 'sniper_premium' in ensemble. Consider disabling if <30% WR persists over 20+ trades.
